methods.description: Process for preparing and selecting rock samples for K-Ar dating by examining their textural and petrographic characteristics to ensure minimal alteration.

steps.description: Thin section examination was carried out on sampled rocks to identify their textural and petrographic characteristics and to select for dating only those that did not suffer significant alteration.
steps.searchable_summary: examine rock samples for textural and petrographic characteristics

methods.description: Process of crushing and sieving rock samples to obtain the appropriate size fraction for separating groundmass from phenocrysts.

steps.description: The samples were crushed and sieved to the adequate size fraction (63-125 µm) defined for each sample to allow the separation of groundmass from the possibly inherited phenocrysts.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and sieve rock samples to 63-125 µm

methods.description: Chemical cleaning and separation of groundmass fractions using acid digestion and physical separation techniques.

steps.description: It was followed by a 10% HNO3 acid digestion and cleaning with ionized water in ultrasonic bath.
steps.searchable_summary: perform acid digestion and ultrasonic cleaning

steps.description: The groundmass fraction was separated from the other phases in heavy liquid and magnetic separator.
steps.searchable_summary: separate groundmass using heavy liquid and magnetic separator

steps.description: The homogeneity and purity of the final fraction were checked under the binocular microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: check homogeneity and purity under microscope

methods.description: Application of the unspiked Cassignol-Gillot K-Ar technique for dating volcanic rocks, involving independent K and Ar measurements.

steps.description: Independent K and Ar measurements were performed on the separated, unaltered groundmass fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform K and Ar measurements on groundmass fractions

steps.description: Following dissolution using a mixture of hydrofluoric, nitric and perchloric acids to break the silicate network, whole potassium content (K) was measured by an Agilent 240 Series AA flame adsorption spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure potassium content using flame adsorption spectrometer

steps.description: Ar isotopic measurements were performed using a multi-collector 180° sector mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Ar isotopic measurements using mass spectrometer

steps.description: To minimize the effect of mass discrimination, the enrichment of radiogenic argon is calculated from a direct comparison between the instrumental 40 Ar/ 36 Ar sample ratio and the instrumental 40 Ar/ 36 Ar atmospheric ratio at identical pressure immediately after the measurement of Ar isotopic abundance of the sample.
At least two independent age determinations were realized for each sample and the final age is obtained as the mean of individual age determinations.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean age from independent determinations
